18: # Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19: 
20: class AutoCompletesController < ApplicationController
21:   before_action :find_project
22: 
23:   def issues
24:     issues = []
25:     q = (params[:q] || params[:term]).to_s.strip
26:     status = params[:status].to_s
27:     issue_id = params[:issue_id].to_s
28: 
29:     scope = Issue.cross_project_scope(@project, params[:scope]).includes(:tracker).visible
30:     scope = scope.open(status == 'o') if status.present?
31:     scope = scope.where.not(:id => issue_id.to_i) if issue_id.present?
32:     if q.present?
33:       if q =~ /\A#?(\d+)\z/
34:         issues << scope.find_by(:id => $1.to_i)
35:       end
36:       issues += scope.like(q).order(:id => :desc).limit(10).to_a
37:       issues.compact!
38:     else
39:       issues += scope.order(:id => :desc).limit(10).to_a
40:     end
41: 
42:     render :json => format_issues_json(issues)
43:   end
44: 
45:   def wiki_pages
46:     q = params[:q].to_s.strip
47:     wiki = Wiki.find_by(project: @project)
48:     if wiki.nil? || !User.current.allowed_to?(:view_wiki_pages, @project)
49:       render json: []
50:       return
51:     end
52: 
53:     scope = wiki.pages.reorder(id: :desc)
54:     wiki_pages =
55:       if q.present?
56:         scope.where("LOWER(#{WikiPage.table_name}.title) LIKE LOWER(?)", "%#{q}%").limit(10).to_a
57:       else
58:         scope.limit(10).to_a
59:       end
60:     render json: format_wiki_pages_json(wiki_pages)
61:   end
62: 
63:   private
64: 
65:   def find_project
66:     if params[:project_id].present?
67:       @project = Project.find(params[:project_id])
68:     end
69:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
70:     render_404
71:   end
72: 
73:   def format_issues_json(issues)
74:     issues.map do |issue|
75:       {
76:         'id' => issue.id,
77:         'label' => "#{issue.tracker} ##{issue.id}: #{issue.subject.to_s.truncate(255)}",
78:         'value' => issue.id
79:       }
80:     end
81:   end
82: 
83:   def format_wiki_pages_json(wiki_pages)
84:     wiki_pages.map do |wiki_page|
85:       {
86:         'id' => wiki_page.id,
87:         'label' => wiki_page.title.to_s.truncate(255),
88:         'value' => wiki_page.title
89:       }
90:     end
91:   end
92: end

19: 
20: class IssueRelationsController < ApplicationController
21:   helper :issues
22: 
23:   before_action :find_issue, :authorize, :only => [:index, :create]
24:   before_action :find_relation, :only => [:show, :destroy]
25: 
26:   accept_api_auth :index, :show, :create, :destroy
27: 
28:   def index
29:     @relations = @issue.relations
30: 
31:     respond_to do |format|
32:       format.html {head :ok}
33:       format.api
34:     end
35:   end
36: 
37:   def show
38:     raise Unauthorized unless @relation.visible?
39: 
40:     respond_to do |format|
41:       format.html {head :ok}
42:       format.api
43:     end
44:   end
45: 
46:   def create
47:     saved = false
48:     params_relation = params[:relation]
49:     unsaved_relations = []
50: 
51:     relation_issues_to_id.each do |issue_to_id|
52:       params_relation[:issue_to_id] = issue_to_id
53: 
54:       @relation = IssueRelation.new
55:       @relation.issue_from = @issue
56:       @relation.safe_attributes = params_relation
57:       @relation.init_journals(User.current)
58: 
59:       begin
60:         saved = @relation.save
61:       rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
62:         @relation.errors.add :base, :taken
63:       end
64: 
65:       unsaved_relations << @relation unless saved
66:     end
67: 
68:     respond_to do |format|
69:       format.html {redirect_to issue_path(@issue)}
70:       format.js do
71:         @relations = select_relations(@issue)
72:         @unsaved_relations = unsaved_relations
73:       end
74:       format.api do
75:         if saved
76:           render :action => 'show', :status => :created, :location => relation_url(@relation)
77:         else
78:           render_validation_errors(@relation)
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82:   end
83: 
84:   def destroy
85:     raise Unauthorized unless @relation.deletable?
86: 
87:     @relation.init_journals(User.current)
88:     @relation.destroy
89: 
90:     respond_to do |format|
91:       format.html {redirect_to issue_path(@relation.issue_from)}
92:       format.js do
93:         find_issue
94:         @relations = select_relations(@issue)
95:       end
96:       format.api  {render_api_ok}
97:     end
98:   end
99: 
100:   private
101: 
102:   def find_issue
103:     @issue = Issue.find(params[:issue_id])
104:     @project = @issue.project
105:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
106:     render_404
107:   end
108: 
109:   def find_relation
110:     @relation = IssueRelation.find(params[:id])
111:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
112:     render_404
113:   end
114: 
115:   def relation_issues_to_id
116:     issue_to_id = params[:relation].require(:issue_to_id)
117:     case issue_to_id
118:     when String
119:       issue_to_id = issue_to_id.split(',').reject(&:blank?)
120:     when Integer
121:       issue_to_id = [issue_to_id]
122:     end
123:     issue_to_id
124:   rescue ActionController::ParameterMissing => e
125:     # We return a empty array just to loop once and return a validation error
126:     # ToDo: Find a better method to return an error if the param is missing.
127:     ['']
128:   end
129: 
130:   def select_relations(issue)
131:     issue.reload.relations.select {|r| r.other_issue(issue) && r.other_issue(issue).visible?}
132:   end
133: end

19: 
20: class SearchController < ApplicationController
21:   before_action :find_optional_project_by_id, :authorize_global
22:   accept_api_auth :index
23: 
24:   def index
25:     @question = params[:q]&.strip || ""
26:     @all_words = params[:all_words] ? params[:all_words].present? : true
27:     @titles_only = params[:titles_only] ? params[:titles_only].present? : false
28:     @search_attachments = params[:attachments].presence || '0'
29:     @open_issues = params[:open_issues] ? params[:open_issues].present? : false
30: 
31:     case params[:format]
32:     when 'xml', 'json'
33:       @offset, @limit = api_offset_and_limit
34:     else
35:       @offset = nil
36:       @limit = Setting.search_results_per_page.to_i
37:       @limit = 10 if @limit == 0
38:     end
39: 
40:     # quick jump to an issue
41:     if !api_request? && (m = @question.match(/^#?(\d+)$/)) && (issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(m[1].to_i))
42:       redirect_to issue_path(issue)
43:       return
44:     end
45: 
46:     projects_to_search =
47:       case params[:scope]
48:       when 'all'
49:         nil
50:       when 'my_projects'
51:         User.current.projects
52:       when 'bookmarks'
53:         Project.where(id: User.current.bookmarked_project_ids)
54:       when 'subprojects'
55:         @project ? (@project.self_and_descendants.to_a) : nil
56:       else
57:         @project
58:       end
59: 
60:     @object_types = Redmine::Search.available_search_types.dup
61:     if projects_to_search.is_a? Project
62:       # don't search projects
63:       @object_types.delete('projects')
64:       # only show what the user is allowed to view
65:       @object_types = @object_types.select {|o| User.current.allowed_to?(:"view_#{o}", projects_to_search)}
66:     end
67: 
68:     @scope = @object_types.select {|t| params[t].present?}
69:     @scope = @object_types if @scope.empty?
70: 
71:     fetcher = Redmine::Search::Fetcher.new(
72:       @question, User.current, @scope, projects_to_search,
73:       :all_words => @all_words, :titles_only => @titles_only, :attachments => @search_attachments, :open_issues => @open_issues,
74:       :cache => params[:page].present?, :params => params.to_unsafe_hash
75:     )
76: 
77:     if fetcher.tokens.present?
78:       @result_count = fetcher.result_count
79:       @result_count_by_type = fetcher.result_count_by_type
80:       @tokens = fetcher.tokens

29:       @issue = @issues.first

32: 
33:     @allowed_statuses = @issues.map(&:new_statuses_allowed_to).reduce(:&)

35:     @can = {
36:       :edit => @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?),

39:       :add_watchers => User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects),

42:     }

44:     @assignables = @issues.map(&:assignable_users).reduce(:&)
45:     @trackers = @projects.map {|p| Issue.allowed_target_trackers(p)}.reduce(:&)

48:     @priorities = IssuePriority.active.reverse
49:     @back = back_url

53:       if relative_url_root.present? && back_url&.starts_with?(relative_url_root)
54:         normalized_back_url = back_url.delete_prefix(relative_url_root)
55:       else
56:         normalized_back_url = back_url
57:       end

59:       @include_delete =

61:           {controller: 'issues', action: 'index'},

67:     end

69:     @columns = params[:c]

72:     if @can[:edit]
73:       custom_fields = @issues.map(&:editable_custom_fields).reduce(:&).reject(&:multiple?).select {|field| field.format.bulk_edit_supported}
74:       custom_fields.each do |field|
75:         values = field.possible_values_options(@projects)
76:         if values.present?
77:           @options_by_custom_field[field] = values
78:         end
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     @safe_attributes = @issues.map(&:safe_attribute_names).reduce(:&)
83:     render :layout => false
84:   end

88:       preload(:project => :time_entry_activities).

92:       render_404;
93:       return
94:     end
95: 
96:     if @time_entries.size == 1
97:       @time_entry = @time_entries.first

100:     @projects = @time_entries.filter_map(&:project).uniq

103: 
104:     edit_allowed = @time_entries.all? {|t| t.editable_by?(User.current)}
105:     @can = {:edit => edit_allowed, :delete => edit_allowed}
106:     @back = back_url
107: 
108:     @options_by_custom_field = {}
109:     if @can[:edit]
110:       custom_fields = @time_entries.map(&:editable_custom_fields).reduce(:&).reject(&:multiple?).select {|field| field.format.bulk_edit_supported}
111:       custom_fields.each do |field|
112:         values = field.possible_values_options(@projects)
113:         if values.present?
114:           @options_by_custom_field[field] = values
115:         end
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     render :layout => false
120:   end
121: 
122:   def projects
123:     @projects = Project.where(id: params[:ids]).to_a
124:     if @projects.empty?
125:       render_404
126:       return
127:     end
128: 
129:     if @projects.size == 1
130:       @project = @projects.first
131:     end
132:     render layout: false
133:   end
134: 
135:   def users
136:     @users = User.where(id: params[:ids]).to_a
137: 
138:     (render_404; return) unless @users.present?
139:     if @users.size == 1
140:       @user = @users.first
141:     end
142: 
143:     @groups = Group.givable.sorted.to_a
144:     @common_group_ids = Group.givable.joins(:groups_users).where(groups_users: { user_id: @users.map(&:id) }).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set
145:     @back = back_url
146: 
147:     render layout: false
148:   end
149: end

524:   # Returns the textual representation of a single journal detail
525:   def show_detail(detail, no_html=false, options={})
526:     multiple = false
527:     show_diff = false
528:     no_details = false
529: 
530:     case detail.property
531:     when 'attr'
532:       field = detail.prop_key.to_s.delete_suffix('_id')
533:       label = l(("field_#{field}").to_sym)
534:       case detail.prop_key
535:       when 'due_date', 'start_date'
536:         value = format_date(detail.value.to_date) if detail.value
537:         old_value = format_date(detail.old_value.to_date) if detail.old_value
538: 
539:       when 'project_id', 'status_id', 'tracker_id', 'assigned_to_id',
540:             'priority_id', 'category_id', 'fixed_version_id'
541:         value = find_name_by_reflection(field, detail.value)
542:         old_value = find_name_by_reflection(field, detail.old_value)
543: 
544:       when 'estimated_hours'
545:         value = l_hours_short(detail.value.to_f) unless detail.value.blank?
546:         old_value = l_hours_short(detail.old_value.to_f) unless detail.old_value.blank?
547: 
548:       when 'parent_id'
549:         label = l(:field_parent_issue)
550:         value = "##{detail.value}" unless detail.value.blank?
551:         old_value = "##{detail.old_value}" unless detail.old_value.blank?
552: 
553:       when 'child_id'
554:         label = l(:label_subtask)
555:         value = "##{detail.value}" unless detail.value.blank?
556:         old_value = "##{detail.old_value}" unless detail.old_value.blank?
557:         multiple = true
558: 
559:       when 'is_private'
560:         value = l(detail.value == "0" ? :general_text_No : :general_text_Yes) unless detail.value.blank?
561:         old_value = l(detail.old_value == "0" ? :general_text_No : :general_text_Yes) unless detail.old_value.blank?
562: 
563:       when 'description'
564:         show_diff = true
565:       end
566:     when 'cf'
567:       custom_field = detail.custom_field
568:       if custom_field
569:         label = custom_field.name
570:         if custom_field.format.class.change_no_details
571:           no_details = true
572:         elsif custom_field.format.class.change_as_diff
573:           show_diff = true
574:         else
575:           multiple = custom_field.multiple?
576:           value = format_value(detail.value, custom_field) if detail.value
577:           old_value = format_value(detail.old_value, custom_field) if detail.old_value
578:         end
579:       end
580:     when 'attachment'
581:       label = l(:label_attachment)
582:     when 'relation'
583:       if detail.value && !detail.old_value
584:         rel_issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(detail.value)
585:         value =
586:           if rel_issue.nil?
587:             "#{l(:label_issue)} ##{detail.value}"
588:           else
589:             (no_html ? rel_issue : link_to_issue(rel_issue, :only_path => options[:only_path]))
590:           end
591:       elsif detail.old_value && !detail.value
592:         rel_issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(detail.old_value)
593:         old_value =
594:           if rel_issue.nil?
595:             "#{l(:label_issue)} ##{detail.old_value}"
596:           else
597:             (no_html ? rel_issue : link_to_issue(rel_issue, :only_path => options[:only_path]))
598:           end
599:       end
600:       relation_type = IssueRelation::TYPES[detail.prop_key]
601:       label = l(relation_type[:name]) if relation_type
602:     end
603:     call_hook(:helper_issues_show_detail_after_setting,
604:               {:detail => detail, :label => label, :value => value, :old_value => old_value})
605: 
606:     label ||= detail.prop_key
607:     value ||= detail.value
608:     old_value ||= detail.old_value
609: 
610:     unless no_html
611:       label = content_tag('strong', label)
612:       old_value = content_tag("i", h(old_value)) if detail.old_value
613:       if detail.old_value && detail.value.blank? && detail.property != 'relation'
614:         old_value = content_tag("del", old_value)
615:       end
616:       if detail.property == 'attachment' && value.present? &&
617:           atta = detail.journal.journalized.attachments.detect {|a| a.id == detail.prop_key.to_i}
618:         # Link to the attachment if it has not been removed
619:         value = link_to_attachment(atta, only_path: options[:only_path])
620:         if options[:only_path] != false
621:           value += ' '
622:           value += link_to_attachment atta, class: 'icon-only icon-download', title: l(:button_download), download: true, icon: 'download'
623:         end
624:       else
625:         value = content_tag("i", h(value)) if value
626:       end
627:     end
628: 
629:     if no_details
630:       s = l(:text_journal_changed_no_detail, :label => label).html_safe
631:     elsif show_diff
632:       s = l(:text_journal_changed_no_detail, :label => label)
633:       unless no_html
634:         diff_link =
635:           link_to(
636:             l(:label_diff),
637:             diff_journal_url(detail.journal_id, :detail_id => detail.id,
638:                              :only_path => options[:only_path]),
639:             :title => l(:label_view_diff))
640:         s << " (#{diff_link})"
641:       end
642:       s.html_safe
643:     elsif detail.value.present?
644:       case detail.property
645:       when 'attr', 'cf'
646:         if detail.old_value.present?
647:           l(:text_journal_changed, :label => label, :old => old_value, :new => value).html_safe
648:         elsif multiple
649:           l(:text_journal_added, :label => label, :value => value).html_safe
650:         else
651:           l(:text_journal_set_to, :label => label, :value => value).html_safe
652:         end
653:       when 'attachment', 'relation'
654:         l(:text_journal_added, :label => label, :value => value).html_safe
655:       end
656:     else
657:       l(:text_journal_deleted, :label => label, :old => old_value).html_safe
658:     end
659:   end

672:           name = record.name.force_encoding('UTF-8')
673:         end
674:       end
675:       hash[key] = name
676:     end
677:     @detail_value_name_by_reflection[[field, id]]
678:   end

264:   # Helper that formats object for html or text rendering
265:   # Options:
266:   # * :html - If true, format the object as HTML (default: true)
267:   # * :thousands_delimiter - If true, format the numeric object with thousands delimiter (default: false)
268:   def format_object(object, *args, &block)
269:     options =
270:       if args.first.is_a?(Hash)
271:         args.first
272:       elsif !args.empty?
273:         # Support the old syntax `format_object(object, html_flag)`
274:         # TODO: Display a deprecation warning in a future version, then remove this
275:         {:html => args.first}
276:       else
277:         {}
278:       end
279: 
280:     html = options.fetch(:html, true)
281:     thousands_delimiter = options.fetch(:thousands_delimiter, false)
282:     delimiter_char = thousands_delimiter ? ::I18n.t('number.format.delimiter') : nil
283: 
284:     if block
285:       object = yield object
286:     end
287:     case object
288:     when Array
289:       formatted_objects = object.map do |o|
290:         format_object(o, html: html, thousands_delimiter: thousands_delimiter)
291:       end
292:       html ? safe_join(formatted_objects, ', ') : formatted_objects.join(', ')
293:     when Time, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone
294:       format_time(object)
295:     when Date
296:       format_date(object)
297:     when Integer
298:       number_with_delimiter(object, delimiter: delimiter_char)
299:     when Float
300:       number_with_delimiter(sprintf('%.2f', object), delimiter: delimiter_char)
301:     when User, Group
302:       html ? link_to_principal(object) : object.to_s
303:     when Project
304:       html ? link_to_project(object) : object.to_s
305:     when Version
306:       html ? link_to_version(object) : object.to_s
307:     when TrueClass
308:       l(:general_text_Yes)
309:     when FalseClass

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2:

17: # along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
18: # Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19: 
20: class Changeset < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :repository
22:   belongs_to :user
23:   has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :dependent => :delete_all
24:   has_and_belongs_to_many :issues
25:   has_and_belongs_to_many :parents,
26:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
27:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
28:                           :association_foreign_key => 'parent_id', :foreign_key => 'changeset_id'
29:   has_and_belongs_to_many :children,
30:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
31:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
32:                           :association_foreign_key => 'changeset_id', :foreign_key => 'parent_id'
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title => proc {|o| o.title},
36:     :description => :long_comments,
37:     :datetime => :committed_on,
38:     :url =>
39:       proc do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'repositories', :action => 'revision',
41:          :id => o.repository.project,
42:          :repository_id => o.repository.identifier_param, :rev => o.identifier}
43:       end
44:   )
45:   acts_as_searchable :columns => 'comments',
46:                      :preload => {:repository => :project},
47:                      :project_key => "#{Repository.table_name}.project_id",
48:                      :date_column => :committed_on
49: 
50:   acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.committed_on",
51:                             :author_key => :user_id,
52:                             :scope => proc {preload(:user, {:repository => :project})}
53: 
54:   validates_presence_of :repository_id, :revision, :committed_on, :commit_date
55:   validates_uniqueness_of :revision, :scope => :repository_id, :case_sensitive => true
56:   validates_uniqueness_of :scmid, :scope => :repository_id, :allow_nil => true, :case_sensitive => true
57: 
58:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
59:     joins(:repository => :project).
60:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(args.shift || User.current, :view_changesets, *args))
61:   end)
62: 
63:   before_create :before_create_cs
64:   after_create :scan_for_issues
65: 
66:   def revision=(r)
67:     write_attribute :revision, (r.nil? ? nil : r.to_s)
68:   end
69: 
70:   # Returns the identifier of this changeset; depending on repository backends
71:   def identifier
72:     if repository.class.respond_to? :changeset_identifier
73:       repository.class.changeset_identifier self
74:     else
75:       revision.to_s
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def committed_on=(date)
80:     self.commit_date = date
81:     super
82:   end
83: 
84:   # Returns the readable identifier
85:   def format_identifier
86:     if repository.class.respond_to? :format_changeset_identifier
87:       repository.class.format_changeset_identifier self
88:     else
89:       identifier
90:     end
91:   end
92: 
93:   def project
94:     repository.project
95:   end
96: 
97:   def author
98:     user || committer.to_s.split('<').first
99:   end
100: 
101:   def before_create_cs
102:     self.committer = self.class.to_utf8(self.committer, repository.repo_log_encoding)
103:     self.comments =
104:       self.class.normalize_comments(self.comments, repository.repo_log_encoding)
105:     self.user = repository.find_committer_user(self.committer)
106:   end
107: 
108:   def scan_for_issues
109:     scan_comment_for_issue_ids
110:   end
111: 
112:   TIMELOG_RE = /
113:     (
114:     ((\d+)(h|hours?))((\d+)(m|min)?)?
115:     |
116:     ((\d+)(h|hours?|m|min))
117:     |
118:     (\d+):(\d+)
119:     |
120:     (\d+([.,]\d+)?)h?
121:     )
122:     /x
123: 
124:   def scan_comment_for_issue_ids
125:     return if comments.blank?
126: 
127:     # keywords used to reference issues
128:     ref_keywords = Setting.commit_ref_keywords.downcase.split(",").collect(&:strip)
129:     ref_keywords_any = ref_keywords.delete('*')
130: 
131:     # keywords used to fix issues
132:     fix_keywords = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.pluck('keywords').flatten.compact
133:     kw_regexp = (ref_keywords + fix_keywords).collect{|kw| Regexp.escape(kw)}.join("|")
134: 
135:     referenced_issues = []
136:     regexp =
137:       %r{
138:         ([\s(\[,-]|^)((#{kw_regexp})[\s:]+)?
139:         (\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?([\s,;&]+\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?)*)
140:         (?=[[:punct:]]|\s|<|$)
141:       }xi
142:     comments.scan(regexp) do |match|
143:       action = match[2].to_s.downcase
144:       refs   = match[3]
145:       next unless action.present? || ref_keywords_any
146: 
147:       refs.scan(/#(\d+)(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?/o).each do |m|
148:         issue = find_referenced_issue_by_id(m[0].to_i)
149:         hours = m[2]
150:         if issue && !issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
151:           referenced_issues << issue
152:           # Don't update issues or log time when importing old commits
153:           unless repository.created_on && committed_on && committed_on < repository.created_on
154:             fix_issue(issue, action) if fix_keywords.include?(action)
155:             log_time(issue, hours) if hours && Setting.commit_logtime_enabled?
156:           end
157:         end
158:       end
159:     end
160: 
161:     referenced_issues.uniq!
162:     self.issues = referenced_issues unless referenced_issues.empty?
163:   end
164: 
165:   def short_comments
166:     @short_comments || split_comments.first
167:   end
168: 
169:   def long_comments
170:     @long_comments || split_comments.last
171:   end
172: 
173:   def text_tag(ref_project=nil)
174:     repo = ""
175:     if repository && repository.identifier.present?
176:       repo = "#{repository.identifier}|"
177:     end
178:     tag = scmid? ? "commit:#{repo}#{scmid}" : "#{repo}r#{revision}"
179:     if ref_project && project && ref_project != project
180:       tag = "#{project.identifier}:#{tag}"
181:     end
182:     tag
183:   end
184: 
185:   # Returns the title used for the changeset in the activity/search results
186:   def title
187:     repo = (repository && repository.identifier.present?) ? " (#{repository.identifier})" : ''
188:     comm = short_comments.blank? ? '' : (': ' + short_comments)
189:     "#{l(:label_revision)} #{format_identifier}#{repo}#{comm}"
190:   end
191: 
192:   # Returns the previous changeset
193:   def previous
194:     @previous ||= repository&.previous_changeset(self)
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Returns the next changeset
198:   def next
199:     @next ||= repository&.next_changeset(self)
200:   end
201: 
202:   # Creates a new Change from it's common parameters
203:   def create_change(change)
204:     Change.create(:changeset     => self,
205:                   :action        => change[:action],
206:                   :path          => change[:path],
207:                   :from_path     => change[:from_path],
208:                   :from_revision => change[:from_revision])
209:   end
210: 
211:   # Finds an issue that can be referenced by the commit message
212:   def find_referenced_issue_by_id(id)
213:     return nil if id.blank?
214: 
215:     issue = Issue.find_by_id(id.to_i)
216:     if Setting.commit_cross_project_ref?
217:       # all issues can be referenced/fixed
218:     elsif issue
219:       # issue that belong to the repository project, a subproject or a parent project only
220:       unless issue.project &&
221:                 (project == issue.project || project.is_ancestor_of?(issue.project) ||
222:                  project.is_descendant_of?(issue.project))
223:         issue = nil
224:       end
225:     end
226:     issue
227:   end
228: 
229:   private
230: 
231:   # Returns true if the issue is already linked to the same commit
232:   # from a different repository
233:   def issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
234:     repository.same_commits_in_scope(issue.changesets, self).any?
235:   end
236: 
237:   # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
238:   def fix_issue(issue, action)
239:     # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)
240:     issue.reload
241:     # don't change the status is the issue is closed
242:     return if issue.closed?
243: 
244:     journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,
245:                                  ll(Setting.default_language,
246:                                     :text_status_changed_by_changeset,
247:                                     text_tag(issue.project)))
248:     rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
249:       rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
250:         (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)
251:     end
252:     if rule
253:       issue.assign_attributes rule.slice(*Issue.attribute_names)
254:     end
255:     Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_changeset_scan_commit_for_issue_ids_pre_issue_update,
256:                             {:changeset => self, :issue => issue, :action => action})
257: 
258:     if issue.changes.any?
259:       unless issue.save
260:         logger.warn("Issue ##{issue.id} could not be saved by changeset #{id}: #{issue.errors.full_messages}") if logger
261:       end
262:     else
263:       issue.clear_journal
264:     end
265:     issue
266:   end
267: 
268:   def log_time(issue, hours)
269:     time_entry =
270:       TimeEntry.new(
271:         :user => user,
272:         :hours => hours,
273:         :issue => issue,
274:         :spent_on => commit_date,
275:         :comments => l(:text_time_logged_by_changeset, :value => text_tag(issue.project),
276:                        :locale => Setting.default_language)
277:       )
278:     if activity = issue.project.commit_logtime_activity
279:       time_entry.activity = activity
280:     end
281: 
282:     unless time_entry.save
283:       logger.warn("TimeEntry could not be created by changeset #{id}: #{time_entry.errors.full_messages}") if logger
284:     end
285:     time_entry
286:   end
287: 
288:   def split_comments
289:     comments =~ /\A(.+?)\r?\n(.*)$/m
290:     @short_comments = $1 || comments
291:     @long_comments = $2.to_s.strip
292:     [@short_comments, @long_comments]
293:   end
294: 
295:   # Singleton class method is public
296:   class << self
297:     # Strips and reencodes a commit log before insertion into the database
298:     def normalize_comments(str, encoding)
299:       Changeset.to_utf8(str.to_s, encoding).strip
300:     end
301: 
302:     def to_utf8(str, encoding)
303:       Redmine::CodesetUtil.to_utf8(str, encoding)
304:     end
305:   end
306: end

22:   has_and_belongs_to_many :trackers, :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_trackers#{table_name_suffix}", :foreign_key => "custom_field_id", :autosave => true

35:   def visibility_by_project_condition(project_key=nil, user=User.current, id_column=nil)
36:     sql = super
37:     id_column ||= id
38:     tracker_condition = "#{Issue.table_name}.tracker_id IN (SELECT tracker_id FROM #{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_trackers#{table_name_suffix} WHERE custom_field_id = #{id_column})"
39:     project_condition = "EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM #{CustomField.table_name} ifa WHERE ifa.is_for_all = #{self.class.connection.quoted_true} AND ifa.id = #{id_column})" +
40:       " OR #{Issue.table_name}.project_id IN (SELECT project_id FROM #{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_projects#{table_name_suffix} WHERE custom_field_id = #{id_column})"
41: 
42:     "((#{sql}) AND (#{tracker_condition}) AND (#{project_condition}) AND (#{Issue.visible_condition(user)}))"
43:   end

46:     super
47:     errors.add(:base, l(:label_role_plural) + ' ' + l('activerecord.errors.messages.blank')) unless visible? || roles.present?

40: 
41:   TYPE_RELATES      = "relates"
42:   TYPE_DUPLICATES   = "duplicates"
43:   TYPE_DUPLICATED   = "duplicated"
44:   TYPE_BLOCKS       = "blocks"
45:   TYPE_BLOCKED      = "blocked"
46:   TYPE_PRECEDES     = "precedes"
47:   TYPE_FOLLOWS      = "follows"
48:   TYPE_COPIED_TO    = "copied_to"
49:   TYPE_COPIED_FROM  = "copied_from"
50: 
51:   TYPES = {
52:     TYPE_RELATES =>     {:name => :label_relates_to, :sym_name => :label_relates_to,
53:                          :order => 1, :sym => TYPE_RELATES},
54:     TYPE_DUPLICATES =>  {:name => :label_duplicates, :sym_name => :label_duplicated_by,
55:                          :order => 2, :sym => TYPE_DUPLICATED},
56:     TYPE_DUPLICATED =>  {:name => :label_duplicated_by, :sym_name => :label_duplicates,
57:                          :order => 3, :sym => TYPE_DUPLICATES, :reverse => TYPE_DUPLICATES},
58:     TYPE_BLOCKS =>      {:name => :label_blocks, :sym_name => :label_blocked_by,
59:                          :order => 4, :sym => TYPE_BLOCKED},
60:     TYPE_BLOCKED =>     {:name => :label_blocked_by, :sym_name => :label_blocks,
61:                          :order => 5, :sym => TYPE_BLOCKS, :reverse => TYPE_BLOCKS},
62:     TYPE_PRECEDES =>    {:name => :label_precedes, :sym_name => :label_follows,
63:                          :order => 6, :sym => TYPE_FOLLOWS},
64:     TYPE_FOLLOWS =>     {:name => :label_follows, :sym_name => :label_precedes,
65:                          :order => 7, :sym => TYPE_PRECEDES, :reverse => TYPE_PRECEDES},
66:     TYPE_COPIED_TO =>   {:name => :label_copied_to, :sym_name => :label_copied_from,
67:                          :order => 8, :sym => TYPE_COPIED_FROM},
68:     TYPE_COPIED_FROM => {:name => :label_copied_from, :sym_name => :label_copied_to,
69:                          :order => 9, :sym => TYPE_COPIED_TO, :reverse => TYPE_COPIED_TO}
70:   }.freeze
71: 
72:   validates_presence_of :issue_from, :issue_to, :relation_type
73:   validates_inclusion_of :relation_type, :in => TYPES.keys
74:   validates_numericality_of :delay, :allow_nil => true
75:   validates_uniqueness_of :issue_to_id, :scope => :issue_from_id, :case_sensitive => true
76:   validate :validate_issue_relation
77: 
78:   before_save :handle_issue_order
79:   after_create  :call_issues_relation_added_callback
80:   after_destroy :call_issues_relation_removed_callback
81: 
82:   safe_attributes 'relation_type',
83:                   'delay',
84:                   'issue_to_id'
85: 
86:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
87:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
88:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
89:     end
90:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
91: 
92:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
93:     if issue_id = attrs.delete('issue_to_id')
94:       if issue_id.to_s.strip =~ /\A#?(\d+)\z/
95:         issue_id = $1.to_i
96:         self.issue_to = Issue.visible(user).find_by_id(issue_id)
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     super(attrs)
101:   end
102: 
103:   def visible?(user=User.current)
104:     (issue_from.nil? || issue_from.visible?(user)) && (issue_to.nil? || issue_to.visible?(user))
105:   end
106: 
107:   def deletable?(user=User.current)
108:     visible?(user) &&
109:       ((issue_from.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_from.project)) ||
110:         (issue_to.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_to.project)))
111:   end
112: 
113:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
114:     super
115:     if new_record?
116:       if relation_type.blank?
117:         self.relation_type = IssueRelation::TYPE_RELATES
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   def validate_issue_relation
123:     if issue_from && issue_to
124:       errors.add :issue_to_id, :invalid if issue_from_id == issue_to_id
125:       unless issue_from.project_id == issue_to.project_id ||
126:                 Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
127:         errors.add :issue_to_id, :not_same_project
128:       end
129:       if circular_dependency?
130:         errors.add :base, :circular_dependency
131:       end
132:       if issue_from.is_descendant_of?(issue_to) || issue_from.is_ancestor_of?(issue_to)
133:         errors.add :base, :cant_link_an_issue_with_a_descendant
134:       end
135:     end
136:   end
137: 
138:   def other_issue(issue)
139:     (self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? issue_to : issue_from
140:   end
141: 
142:   # Returns the relation type for +issue+
143:   def relation_type_for(issue)
144:     if TYPES[relation_type]
145:       if self.issue_from_id == issue.id
146:         relation_type
147:       else
148:         TYPES[relation_type][:sym]
149:       end
150:     end
151:   end
152: 
153:   def label_for(issue)
154:     if TYPES[relation_type]
155:       TYPES[relation_type][(self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? :name : :sym_name]
156:     else
157:       :unknow
158:     end
159:   end
160: 
161:   def to_s(issue=nil)
162:     issue ||= issue_from
163:     issue_text = block_given? ? yield(other_issue(issue)) : "##{other_issue(issue).try(:id)}"
164:     s = []
165:     s << l(label_for(issue))
166:     s << "(#{l('datetime.distance_in_words.x_days', :count => delay)})" if delay && delay != 0
167:     s << issue_text
168:     s.join(' ')
169:   end
170: 
171:   def css_classes_for(issue)
172:     "rel-#{relation_type_for(issue)}"
173:   end
174: 
175:   def handle_issue_order
176:     reverse_if_needed
177: 
178:     if relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES
179:       self.delay ||= 0
180:     else
181:       self.delay = nil
182:     end
183:     set_issue_to_dates
184:   end
185: 
186:   def set_issue_to_dates(journal=nil)
187:     soonest_start = self.successor_soonest_start
188:     if soonest_start && issue_to
189:       issue_to.reschedule_on!(soonest_start, journal)
190:     end
191:   end
192: 
193:   def successor_soonest_start
194:     if (self.relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES) && delay && issue_from &&
195:            (issue_from.start_date || issue_from.due_date)
196:       add_working_days((issue_from.due_date || issue_from.start_date), (1 + delay))
197:     end
198:   end
199: 
200:   def <=>(relation)
201:     return nil unless relation.is_a?(IssueRelation)
202: 
203:     r = TYPES[self.relation_type][:order] <=> TYPES[relation.relation_type][:order]
204:     r == 0 ? id <=> relation.id : r
205:   end
206: 
207:   def init_journals(user)
208:     issue_from.init_journal(user) if issue_from
209:     issue_to.init_journal(user) if issue_to
210:   end
211: 
212:   private
213: 
214:   # Reverses the relation if needed so that it gets stored in the proper way
215:   # Should not be reversed before validation so that it can be displayed back
216:   # as entered on new relation form.
217:   #
218:   # Orders relates relations by ID, so that uniqueness index in DB is triggered
219:   # on concurrent access.
220:   def reverse_if_needed
221:     if TYPES.has_key?(relation_type) && TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
222:       issue_tmp = issue_to
223:       self.issue_to = issue_from
224:       self.issue_from = issue_tmp
225:       self.relation_type = TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
226: 
227:     elsif relation_type == TYPE_RELATES && issue_from_id > issue_to_id
228:       self.issue_to, self.issue_from = issue_from, issue_to
229:     end
230:   end
231: 
232:   # Returns true if the relation would create a circular dependency
233:   def circular_dependency?
234:     case relation_type
235:     when 'follows'
236:       issue_from.would_reschedule? issue_to
237:     when 'precedes'
238:       issue_to.would_reschedule? issue_from
239:     when 'blocked'
240:       issue_from.blocks? issue_to
241:     when 'blocks'
242:       issue_to.blocks? issue_from
243:     when 'relates'
244:       self.class.where(issue_from_id: issue_to, issue_to_id: issue_from).present?
245:     else
246:       false
247:     end
248:   end
249: 
250:   def call_issues_relation_added_callback
251:     call_issues_callback :relation_added
252:   end
253: 
254:   def call_issues_relation_removed_callback
255:     call_issues_callback :relation_removed
256:   end
257: 
258:   def call_issues_callback(name)
259:     [issue_from, issue_to].each do |issue|
260:       if issue
261:         issue.send name, self
262:       end
263:     end
264:   end
265: end

98:   def mappable_custom_fields

100:       issue = Issue.new

113:   def build_object(row, item)
114:     issue = Issue.new
115:     issue.author = user

117: 
118:     tracker_id = nil

120:       tracker_id = tracker.id
121:     elsif tracker_name = row_value(row, 'tracker')
122:       tracker_id = allowed_target_trackers.named(tracker_name).first.try(:id)

126:       'project_id' => mapping['project_id'],

135:     end
136:     issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
137: 
138:     attributes = {}

141:         attributes['priority_id'] = priority_id
142:       end
143:     end
144:     if issue.project && category_name = row_value(row, 'category')

147:       elsif create_categories?

155:     if assignee_name = row_value(row, 'assigned_to')

164:       if version
165:         attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
166:       elsif create_versions?

168:         version.name = version_name
169:         if version.save

172:       end

174:     if is_private = row_value(row, 'is_private')

187:         if issue_id

20: class IssueStatus < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22: 
23:   after_update :handle_is_closed_change
24:   before_destroy :check_integrity
25:   has_many :workflows, :class_name => 'WorkflowTransition', :foreign_key => "old_status_id"
26:   has_many :workflow_transitions_as_new_status, :class_name => 'WorkflowTransition', :foreign_key => "new_status_id"
27:   acts_as_positioned
28: 
29:   before_destroy :delete_workflow_rules
30: 
31:   validates_presence_of :name
32:   validates_uniqueness_of :name, :case_sensitive => true
33:   validates_length_of :name, :maximum => 30
34:   validates_length_of :description, :maximum => 255
35:   validates_inclusion_of :default_done_ratio, :in => 0..100, :allow_nil => true
36: 
37:   scope :sorted, lambda {order(:position)}
38:   scope :named, lambda {|arg| where("LOWER(#{table_name}.name) = LOWER(?)", arg.to_s.strip)}
39: 
40:   safe_attributes(
41:     'name',
42:     'description',
43:     'is_closed',
44:     'position',
45:     'default_done_ratio')
46: 
47:   # Update all the +Issues+ setting their done_ratio to the value of their +IssueStatus+
48:   def self.update_issue_done_ratios
49:     if Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio?
50:       IssueStatus.where("default_done_ratio >= 0").each do |status|
51:         Issue.where({:status_id => status.id}).update_all({:done_ratio => status.default_done_ratio})
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     return Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio?
56:   end
57: 
58:   # Returns an array of all statuses the given role can switch to
59:   def new_statuses_allowed_to(roles, tracker, author=false, assignee=false)
60:     self.class.new_statuses_allowed(self, roles, tracker, author, assignee)
61:   end
62:   alias :find_new_statuses_allowed_to :new_statuses_allowed_to
63: 
64:   def self.new_statuses_allowed(status, roles, tracker, author=false, assignee=false)
65:     if roles.present? && tracker
66:       status_id = status.try(:id) || 0
67: 
68:       scope = IssueStatus.
69:         joins(:workflow_transitions_as_new_status).
70:         where(:workflows => {:old_status_id => status_id, :role_id => roles.map(&:id), :tracker_id => tracker.id})
71: 
72:       unless author && assignee
73:         if author || assignee
74:           scope = scope.where("author = ? OR assignee = ?", author, assignee)
75:         else
76:           scope = scope.where("author = ? AND assignee = ?", false, false)
77:         end
78:       end
79: 
80:       scope.distinct.to_a.sort
81:     else
82:       []
83:     end
84:   end
85: 
86:   def <=>(status)
87:     return nil unless status.is_a?(IssueStatus)
88: 
89:     position <=> status.position
90:   end
91: 
92:   def to_s; name end
93: 
94:   private
95: 
96:   # Updates issues closed_on attribute when an existing status is set as closed.
97:   def handle_is_closed_change
98:     if saved_change_to_is_closed? && is_closed == true
99:       # First we update issues that have a journal for when the current status was set,
100:       # a subselect is used to update all issues with a single query
101:       subquery = Journal.joins(:details).
102:         where(:journalized_type => 'Issue').
103:         where("journalized_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id").
104:         where(:journal_details => {:property => 'attr', :prop_key => 'status_id', :value => id.to_s}).
105:         select("MAX(created_on)").
106:         to_sql
107:       Issue.where(:status_id => id, :closed_on => nil).update_all("closed_on = (#{subquery})")
108: 
109:       # Then we update issues that don't have a journal which means the
110:       # current status was set on creation
111:       Issue.where(:status_id => id, :closed_on => nil).update_all("closed_on = created_on")
112:     end
113:   end
114: 
115:   def check_integrity
116:     if Issue.where(:status_id => id).any?
117:       raise "This status is used by some issues"
118:     elsif Tracker.where(:default_status_id => id).any?
119:       raise "This status is used as the default status by some trackers"
120:     end
121:   end
122: 
123:   # Deletes associated workflows
124:   def delete_workflow_rules
125:     WorkflowRule.where(:old_status_id => id).delete_all
126:     WorkflowRule.where(:new_status_id => id).delete_all
127:   end
128: end

91:   def initialize(*args)

102:   def save(*args)

126:       elsif detail.property == 'relation'

134:   # Returns the JournalDetail for the given attribute, or nil if the attribute

212:   # Sets @custom_field instance variable on journals details using a single query
213:   def self.preload_journals_details_custom_fields(journals)
214:     field_ids = journals.map(&:details).flatten.select {|d| d.property == 'cf'}.map(&:prop_key).uniq
215:     if field_ids.any?
216:       fields_by_id = CustomField.where(:id => field_ids).index_by { |f| f.id }
217:       journals.each do |journal|
218:         journal.details.each do |detail|
219:           if detail.property == 'cf'
220:             detail.instance_variable_set :@custom_field, fields_by_id[detail.prop_key.to_i]
221:           end
222:         end
223:       end
224:     end
225:     journals
226:   end

228:   # Stores the values of the attributes and custom fields of the journalized object
229:   def start
230:     if journalized
231:       @attributes_before_change = journalized.journalized_attribute_names.index_with do |attribute|
232:         journalized.send(attribute)
233:       end
234:       @custom_values_before_change = journalized.custom_field_values.to_h do |c|
235:         [c.custom_field_id, c.value]
236:       end
237:     end
238:     self
239:   end

241:   # Adds a journal detail for an attachment that was added or removed
242:   def journalize_attachment(attachment, added_or_removed)
243:     key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
244:     details <<
245:       JournalDetail.new(
246:         :property => 'attachment',
247:         :prop_key => attachment.id,
248:         key => attachment.filename
249:       )
250:   end
251: 
252:   # Adds a journal detail for an issue relation that was added or removed
253:   def journalize_relation(relation, added_or_removed)
254:     key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
255:     details <<
256:       JournalDetail.new(
257:         :property  => 'relation',
258:         :prop_key  => relation.relation_type_for(journalized),
259:         key => relation.other_issue(journalized).try(:id)
260:       )
261:   end
262: 
263:   private
264: 
265:   # Generates journal details for attribute and custom field changes
266:   def journalize_changes
267:     # attributes changes
268:     if @attributes_before_change
269:       attrs = (journalized.journalized_attribute_names + @attributes_before_change.keys).uniq
270:       attrs.each do |attribute|
271:         before = @attributes_before_change[attribute]
272:         after = journalized.send(attribute)
273:         next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
274: 
275:         add_attribute_detail(attribute, before, after)
276:       end
277:     end
278:     # custom fields changes
279:     if @custom_values_before_change
280:       values_by_custom_field_id = {}
281:       @custom_values_before_change.each_key do |custom_field_id|
282:         values_by_custom_field_id[custom_field_id] = nil
283:       end
284:       journalized.custom_field_values.each do |c|
285:         values_by_custom_field_id[c.custom_field_id] = c.value
286:       end
287: 
288:       values_by_custom_field_id.each do |custom_field_id, after|
289:         before = @custom_values_before_change[custom_field_id]
290:         next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
291: 
292:         if before.is_a?(Array) || after.is_a?(Array)
293:           before = [before] unless before.is_a?(Array)
294:           after = [after] unless after.is_a?(Array)
295: 
296:           # values removed
297:           (before - after).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
298:             add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, value, nil)
299:           end
300:           # values added
301:           (after - before).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
302:             add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, nil, value)
303:           end
304:         else
305:           add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, before, after)
306:         end
307:       end
308:     end
309:     start
310:   end
311: 
312:   # Adds a journal detail for an attribute change
313:   def add_attribute_detail(attribute, old_value, value)
314:     add_detail('attr', attribute, old_value, value)
315:   end
316: 
317:   # Adds a journal detail for a custom field value change
318:   def add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, old_value, value)
319:     add_detail('cf', custom_field_id, old_value, value)
320:   end
321: 
322:   # Adds a journal detail
323:   def add_detail(property, prop_key, old_value, value)
324:     details <<
325:       JournalDetail.new(
326:         :property => property,
327:         :prop_key => prop_key,
328:         :old_value => old_value,
329:         :value => value
330:       )
331:   end
332: 
333:   def split_private_notes
334:     if private_notes?
335:       if notes.present?
336:         if details.any?
337:           # Split the journal (notes/changes) so we don't have half-private journals
338:           journal = Journal.new(:journalized => journalized, :user => user, :notes => nil, :private_notes => false)
339:           journal.details = details
340:           journal.save
341:           self.details = []
342:           self.created_on = journal.created_on
343:         end
344:       else
345:         # Blank notes should not be private
346:         self.private_notes = false
347:       end
348:     end
349:     true
350:   end
351: 
352:   def add_watcher
353:     if user.is_a?(User) &&
354:        user.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_contributed_to') &&
355:        valid_watcher?(user)
356:       journalized.set_watcher(user, true)
357:     end
358: 
359:     assignee = journalized.assigned_to
360:     if assignee.is_a?(User) &&
361:        assignee.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_assigned_to_me') &&
362:        valid_watcher?(assignee)
363:       journalized.set_watcher(assignee, true)
364:     end
365:   end
366: 
367:   def valid_watcher?(user)
368:     user.active? &&
369:       user.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, journalized.project) &&
370:       journalized.valid_watcher?(user) &&
371:       !journalized.watched_by?(user)
372:   end
373: 
374:   def send_notification
375:     if notify? &&
376:         (
377:           Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_updated') ||
378:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_note_added') && notes.present?) ||
379:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_status_updated') && new_status.present?) ||
380:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_assigned_to_updated') && detail_for_attribute('assigned_to_id').present?) ||
381:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_priority_updated') && new_value_for('priority_id').present?) ||
382:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_fixed_version_updated') && detail_for_attribute('fixed_version_id').present?) ||
383:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_attachment_added') && details.any? {|d| d.property == 'attachment' && d.value })
384:         )
385:       Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(self)
386:     end
387:   end
388: 
389:   def select_journal_visible_user(notified)
390:     if private_notes?
391:       notified = notified.select {|user| user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, journalized.project)}
392:     end
393:     notified
394:   end
395: end

120:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
121:     if attrs
122:       attrs = super(attrs)
123:       if issue_id_changed? && issue
124:         if issue.visible?(user) && user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project)
125:           if attrs[:project_id].blank? && issue.project_id != project_id
126:             self.project_id = issue.project_id
127:           end
128:           @invalid_issue_id = nil
129:         elsif user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project) && issue.assigned_to_id_changed? && issue.previous_assignee == User.current
130:           current_assignee = issue.assigned_to
131:           issue.assigned_to = issue.previous_assignee
132:           unless issue.visible?(user)
133:             @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
134:           end
135:           issue.assigned_to = current_assignee
136:         else
137:           @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
138:         end
139:       end
140:       if user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !user.allowed_to?(:log_time_for_other_users, project)
141:         @invalid_user_id = user_id
142:       else
143:         @invalid_user_id = nil
144:       end
145: 
146:       # Delete assigned custom fields not visible by the user
147:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
148:       self.custom_field_values.delete_if do |c|
149:         !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c.custom_field.id.to_s)
150:       end

164:   def validate_time_entry
165:     if hours
166:       errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours < 0
167:       errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours == 0.0 && hours_changed? && !Setting.timelog_accept_0_hours?
168: 
169:       max_hours = Setting.timelog_max_hours_per_day.to_f
170:       if hours_changed? && max_hours > 0.0
171:         logged_hours = other_hours_with_same_user_and_day
172:         if logged_hours + hours > max_hours
173:           errors.add(
174:             :base,
175:             I18n.t(:error_exceeds_maximum_hours_per_day,
176:                    :logged_hours => format_hours(logged_hours),
177:                    :max_hours => format_hours(max_hours)))
178:         end
179:       end
180:     end
181:     errors.add :project_id, :invalid if project.nil?
182:     if @invalid_user_id || (user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !self.assignable_users.map(&:id).include?(user_id))
183:       errors.add :user_id, :invalid
184:     end
185:     errors.add :issue_id, :invalid if (issue_id && !issue) || (issue && project!=issue.project) || @invalid_issue_id
186:     errors.add :activity_id, :inclusion if activity_id_changed? && project && !project.activities.include?(activity)
187:     if spent_on && spent_on_changed? && user
188:       errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_future_date) if !Setting.timelog_accept_future_dates? && (spent_on > user.today)
189:     end
190:     if !Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? && issue&.closed? && issue.was_closed?
191:       errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_closed_issue)
192:     end
193:   end

195:   def hours=(h)
196:     write_attribute :hours, (h.is_a?(String) ? (h.to_hours || h) : h)
197:   end
198: 
199:   def hours
200:     h = read_attribute(:hours)
201:     if h.is_a?(Float)
202:       # Convert the float value to a rational with a denominator of 60 to
203:       # avoid floating point errors.
204:       #
205:       # Examples:
206:       #  0.38333333333333336 => (23/60)   # 23m
207:       #  0.9913888888888889  => (59/60)   # 59m 29s is rounded to 59m
208:       #  0.9919444444444444  => (1/1)     # 59m 30s is rounded to 60m
209:       (h * 60).round / 60r
210:     else
211:       h
212:     end
213:   end

215:   # tyear, tmonth, tweek assigned where setting spent_on attributes
216:   # these attributes make time aggregations easier
217:   def spent_on=(date)
218:     super

596:   # Sends reminders to issue assignees
597:   # Available options:
598:   # * :days     => how many days in the future to remind about (defaults to 7)
599:   # * :tracker  => id of tracker for filtering issues (defaults to all trackers)
600:   # * :project  => id or identifier of project to process (defaults to all projects)
601:   # * :users    => array of user/group ids who should be reminded
602:   # * :version  => name of target version for filtering issues (defaults to none)
603:   def self.reminders(options={})
604:     days = options[:days] || 7
605:     project = options[:project] ? Project.find(options[:project]) : nil
606:     tracker = options[:tracker] ? Tracker.find(options[:tracker]) : nil
607:     target_version_id = options[:version] ? Version.named(options[:version]).pluck(:id) : nil
608:     if options[:version] && target_version_id.blank?
609:       raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound.new("Couldn't find Version named #{options[:version]}")
610:     end
611: 
612:     user_ids = options[:users]
613: 
614:     scope =
615:       Issue.open.where(
616:         "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NOT NULL" \
617:           " AND #{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}" \
618:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.due_date <= ?", days.day.from_now.to_date
619:       )
620:     scope = scope.where(:assigned_to_id => user_ids) if user_ids.present?
621:     scope = scope.where(:project_id => project.id) if project
622:     scope = scope.where(:fixed_version_id => target_version_id) if target_version_id.present?
623:     scope = scope.where(:tracker_id => tracker.id) if tracker
624:     issues_by_assignee = scope.includes(:status, :assigned_to, :project, :tracker).
625:                               group_by(&:assigned_to)
626:     issues_by_assignee.keys.each do |assignee|  # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
627:       if assignee.is_a?(Group)
628:         assignee.users.each do |user|
629:           issues_by_assignee[user] ||= []
630:           issues_by_assignee[user] += issues_by_assignee[assignee]
631:         end
632:       end
633:     end
634: 
635:     issues_by_assignee.each do |assignee, issues|
636:       if assignee.is_a?(User) && assignee.active? && issues.present?
637:         visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}
638:         visible_issues.sort!{|a, b| (a.due_date <=> b.due_date).nonzero? || (a.id <=> b.id)}
639:         reminder(assignee, visible_issues, days).deliver_later if visible_issues.present?
640:       end
641:     end

1086:       wiki = self.wiki || Wiki.new
1087:       wiki.attributes = project.wiki.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1088:       wiki_pages_map = {}
1089:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1090:         # Skip pages without content
1091:         next if page.content.nil?
1092: 
1093:         new_wiki_content =
1094:           WikiContent.new(page.content.attributes.dup.except("id", "page_id", "updated_on"))
1095:         new_wiki_page =
1096:           WikiPage.new(page.attributes.dup.except("id", "wiki_id", "created_on", "parent_id"))
1097:         new_wiki_page.content = new_wiki_content
1098:         wiki.pages << new_wiki_page
1099:         new_wiki_page.attachments =
1100:           page.attachments.map{|attachement| attachement.copy(:container => new_wiki_page)}
1101:         wiki_pages_map[page.id] = new_wiki_page
1102:       end
1103: 
1104:       self.wiki = wiki
1105:       wiki.save
1106:       # Reproduce page hierarchy
1107:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1108:         if page.parent_id && wiki_pages_map[page.id]
1109:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].parent = wiki_pages_map[page.parent_id]
1110:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].save
1111:         end
1112:       end
1113:     end
1114:   end

1155:       new_issue = Issue.new
1156:       new_issue.copy_from(issue, :subtasks => false, :link => false, :keep_status => true)

1164:       if issue.fixed_version && issue.fixed_version.project == project

1166:       end
1167:       # Reassign version custom field values

1172:             if version.project == project
1173:               self.versions.detect {|v| v.name == version.name}.try(:id)

1176:             end
1177:           end
1178:           new_value.compact!
1179:           new_value = new_value.first unless custom_value.custom_field.multiple?

1244:   # Helper method to generate the WHERE sql for a +field+, +operator+ and a +value+
1245:   def sql_for_field(field, operator, value, db_table, db_field, is_custom_filter=false)
1246:     sql = ''
1247:     case operator
1248:     when "="
1249:       if value.any?
1250:         case type_for(field)
1251:         when :date, :date_past
1252:           sql = date_clause(db_table, db_field, parse_date(value.first),
1253:                             parse_date(value.first), is_custom_filter)
1254:         when :integer
1255:           int_values = value.first.to_s.scan(/[+-]?\d+/).map(&:to_i).join(",")
1256:           if int_values.present?
1257:             if is_custom_filter
1258:               sql =
1259:                 "(#{db_table}.#{db_field} <> '' AND " \
1260:                   "CAST(CASE #{db_table}.#{db_field} WHEN '' THEN '0' " \
1261:                     "ELSE #{db_table}.#{db_field} END AS decimal(30,3)) IN (#{int_values}))"
1262:             else
1263:               sql = "#{db_table}.#{db_field} IN (#{int_values})"
1264:             end
1265:           else
1266:             sql = "1=0"
1267:           end
1268:         when :float, :hour
1269:           if is_custom_filter
1270:             sql =
1271:               "(#{db_table}.#{db_field} <> '' AND " \
1272:                 "CAST(CASE #{db_table}.#{db_field} WHEN '' THEN '0' " \
1273:                   "ELSE #{db_table}.#{db_field} END AS decimal(30,3)) " \
1274:                   "BETWEEN #{value.first.to_f - 1e-5} AND #{value.first.to_f + 1e-5})"
1275:           else
1276:             sql = "#{db_table}.#{db_field} BETWEEN #{value.first.to_f - 1e-5} AND #{value.first.to_f + 1e-5}"
1277:           end
1278:         else
1279:           sql = queried_class.send(:sanitize_sql_for_conditions, ["#{db_table}.#{db_field} IN (?)", value])
1280:         end
1281:       else
1282:         # IN an empty set
1283:         sql = "1=0"
1284:       end
1285:     when "!"
1286:       if value.any?
1287:         sql =
1288:           queried_class.send(
1289:             :sanitize_sql_for_conditions,
1290:             ["(#{db_table}.#{db_field} IS NULL OR #{db_table}.#{db_field} NOT IN (?))", value]
1291:           )
1292:       else
1293:         # NOT IN an empty set
1294:         sql = "1=1"
1295:       end
1296:     when "!*"
1297:       sql = "#{db_table}.#{db_field} IS NULL"
1298:       sql += " OR #{db_table}.#{db_field} = ''" if is_custom_filter || [:text, :string].include?(type_for(field))
1299:     when "*"
1300:       sql = "#{db_table}.#{db_field} IS NOT NULL"
1301:       sql += " AND #{db_table}.#{db_field} <> ''" if is_custom_filter || [:text, :string].include?(type_for(field))
1302:     when ">="
1303:       if [:date, :date_past].include?(type_for(field))
1304:         sql = date_clause(db_table, db_field, parse_date(value.first), nil, is_custom_filter)
1305:       else

274:     return false if due_date.nil? || start_date.nil? || completed_percent == 100
275: 
276:     done_date = start_date + ((due_date - start_date + 1) * completed_percent / 100).floor
277:     done_date <= User.current.today
278:   end

281:   # and the time spent on the open issues.
282:   def completed_percent
283:     fixed_issues.completed_percent
284:   end
285: 
286:   # Returns the percentage of issues that have been marked as 'closed'.
287:   def closed_percent
288:     fixed_issues.closed_percent
289:   end
290: 
291:   # Returns true if the version is overdue: due date reached and some open issues
292:   def overdue?
293:     effective_date && (effective_date < User.current.today) && (open_issues_count > 0)
294:   end

162:           :limit => @max_rows

164:       end

166:       # Returns a hash of the relations between the issues that are present on the gantt
167:       # and that should be displayed, grouped by issue ids.
168:       def relations
169:         return @relations if @relations
170: 
171:         if issues.any?
172:           issue_ids = issues.map(&:id)
173:           @relations = IssueRelation.
174:             where(:issue_from_id => issue_ids, :issue_to_id => issue_ids, :relation_type => DRAW_TYPES.keys).
175:             group_by(&:issue_from_id)
176:         else
177:           @relations = {}
178:         end
179:       end
180: 
181:       # Return all the project nodes that will be displayed
182:       def projects
183:         return @projects if @projects
184: 
185:         ids = issues.collect(&:project).uniq.collect(&:id)
186:         if ids.any?
187:           # All issues projects and their visible ancestors
188:           @projects = Project.visible.
189:             joins("LEFT JOIN #{Project.table_name} child ON #{Project.table_name}.lft <= child.lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt >= child.rgt").
190:             where("child.id IN (?)", ids).
191:             order("#{Project.table_name}.lft ASC").
192:             distinct.
193:             to_a
194:         else
195:           @projects = []
196:         end
197:       end

1240:   # Preloads visible spent time for a collection of issues
1241:   def self.load_visible_spent_hours(issues, user=User.current)
1242:     if issues.any?
1243:       hours_by_issue_id = TimeEntry.visible(user).where(:issue_id => issues.map(&:id)).group(:issue_id).sum(:hours)
1244:       issues.each do |issue|
1245:         issue.instance_variable_set :@spent_hours, (hours_by_issue_id[issue.id] || 0.0)
1246:       end
1247:     end

1252:     if issues.any?
1253:       hours_by_issue_id = TimeEntry.visible(user).joins(:issue).
1254:         joins("JOIN #{Issue.table_name} parent ON parent.root_id = #{Issue.table_name}.root_id" +
1255:           " AND parent.lft <= #{Issue.table_name}.lft AND parent.rgt >= #{Issue.table_name}.rgt").
1256:         where("parent.id IN (?)", issues.map(&:id)).group("parent.id").sum(:hours)
1257:       issues.each do |issue|

1259:       end

1270:       # Relations with issue_to in given issues and visible issue_from

1338:   # Returns true if this issue blocks the other issue, otherwise returns false
1339:   def blocks?(other)
1340:     all = [self]
1341:     last = [self]
1342:     while last.any?
1343:       current =
1344:         last.map do |i|
1345:           i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_BLOCKS).map(&:issue_to)
1346:         end.flatten.uniq
1347:       current -= last
1348:       current -= all
1349:       return true if current.include?(other)
1350: 
1351:       last = current
1352:       all += last
1353:     end
1354:     false
1355:   end
1356: 
1357:   # Returns true if the other issue might be rescheduled if the start/due dates of this issue change
1358:   def would_reschedule?(other)
1359:     all = [self]
1360:     last = [self]
1361:     while last.any?
1362:       current = last.map do |i|
1363:         i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to) +
1364:         i.leaves.to_a +
1365:         i.ancestors.map {|a| a.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to)}
1366:       end.flatten.uniq
1367:       current -= last
1368:       current -= all
1369:       return true if current.include?(other)
1370: 
1371:       last = current
1372:       all += last
1373:     end
1374:     false
1375:   end

1573:     if project.nil?
1574:       return Issue
1575:     end
1576: 
1577:     case scope
1578:     when 'all', 'system'
1579:       Issue
1580:     when 'tree'
1581:       Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
1582:                                   :lft => project.root.lft, :rgt => project.root.rgt)
1583:     when 'hierarchy'
1584:       Issue.joins(:project).
1585:         where(
1586:           "(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND " \
1587:             "#{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt) OR " \
1588:             "(#{Project.table_name}.lft < :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > :rgt)",
1589:           :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt
1590:         )
1591:     when 'descendants'
1592:       Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
1593:                                   :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt)
1594:     else
1595:       Issue.where(:project_id => project.id)
1596:     end
1597:   end

20: require 'roadie'
21: 
22: class Mailer < ActionMailer::Base
23:   layout 'mailer'
24:   helper :application
25:   helper :issues
26:   helper :custom_fields
27: 
28:   include Redmine::I18n
29:   include Roadie::Rails::Automatic
30: 
31:   class DeliveryJob < ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob
32:     include Redmine::JobWrapper
33: 
34:     around_enqueue :keep_current_user
35:   end
36: 
37:   self.delivery_job = DeliveryJob
38: 
39:   # Overrides ActionMailer::Base#process in order to set the recipient as the current user
40:   # and his language as the default locale.
41:   # The first argument of all actions of this Mailer must be a User (the recipient),
42:   # otherwise an ArgumentError is raised.
43:   def process(action, *args)
44:     user = args.first
45:     raise ArgumentError, "First argument has to be a user, was #{user.inspect}" unless user.is_a?(User)
46: 
47:     initial_user = User.current
48:     initial_language = ::I18n.locale
49:     begin
50:       User.current = user
51: 
52:       lang = find_language(user.language) if user.logged?
53:       lang ||= Setting.default_language
54:       set_language_if_valid(lang)
55: 
56:       super
57:     ensure
58:       User.current = initial_user
59:       ::I18n.locale = initial_language
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Default URL options for generating URLs in emails based on host_name and protocol
64:   # defined in application settings.
65:   def self.default_url_options
66:     options = {:protocol => Setting.protocol}
67:     if Setting.host_name.to_s =~ /\A(https?\:\/\/)?(.+?)(\:(\d+))?(\/.+)?\z/i
68:       host, port, prefix = $2, $4, $5
69:       options.merge!(
70:         {
71:           :host => host, :port => port, :script_name => prefix
72:         }
73:       )
74:     else
75:       options[:host] = Setting.host_name
76:     end
77:     options
78:   end
79: 
80:   # Builds a mail for notifying user about a new issue
81:   def issue_add(user, issue)
82:     redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
83:                     'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
84:                     'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
85:                     'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
86:                     'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
87:     redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
88:     message_id issue
89:     references issue
90:     @author = issue.author
91:     @issue = issue
92:     @user = user
93:     @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue)
94:     subject = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}]"
95:     subject += " (#{issue.status.name})" if Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
96:     subject += " #{issue.subject}"
97:     mail :to => user,
98:       :subject => subject
99:   end
100: 
101:   # Notifies users about a new issue.
102:   #
103:   # Example:
104:   #   Mailer.deliver_issue_add(issue)
105:   def self.deliver_issue_add(issue)
106:     users = issue.notified_users | issue.notified_watchers | issue.notified_mentions
107:     users.each do |user|
108:       issue_add(user, issue).deliver_later
109:     end
110:   end

118:                     'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
119:                     'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)

19: 
20: class Webhook < ApplicationRecord
21:   Executor = Struct.new(:url, :payload, :secret) do
22:     # @return [Net::HTTP::Response] if the POST request was successful
23:     # @raise [Exception] one of a lot of possible exceptions if the webhook was
24:     #   not successfully send, e.g. if we could not find valid IPs or could not
25:     #   connect to any of them or if an unexpected (i.e. non-successful)
26:     #   response status was set; it may contain the server response.
27:     def call
28:       headers = { accept: '*/*', 'content-type': 'application/json', 'user-agent': 'Redmine' }
29:       if secret.present?
30:         headers['X-Redmine-Signature-256'] = compute_signature
31:       end
32:       Rails.logger.debug { "Webhook: POST #{url}" }
33: 
34:       url = URI.parse(self.url)
35:       valid_ips.each do |ip|
36:         begin
37:           Rails.logger.debug { "Trying #{ip}" }
38:           http = ::Net::HTTP.start(
39:             url.host,
40:             url.port,
41:             open_timeout: 60,
42:             read_timeout: 60,
43:             write_timeout: 60,
44:             use_ssl: (url.scheme == 'https'),
45:             ipaddr: ip
46:           )
47:         rescue
48:           # We could not create a HTTP(S) connection to the IP.
49:           begin
50:             http&.finish
51:           rescue
52:             nil
53:           end
54: 
55:           # Since we have not sent any actual request data, we continue with the
56:           # next IP (if any).
57:           next
58:         end
59: 
60:         request = ::Net::HTTP::Post.new(url, headers)
61:         request.basic_auth url.user, url.password if url.user
62:         request.body = payload
63: 
64:         # Return the response or raise if there was any transport error,
65:         # timeout, or non-successful HTTP response code
66:         return http.request(request).tap(&:value)
67:       ensure
68:         begin
69:           http&.finish
70:         rescue
71:           nil
72:         end
73:       end
74: 
75:       raise SocketError, "Could not connect to any IP"
76:     end
77: 
78:     # Returns a list of IP addresses for the hostname of the URI, or an empty
79:     # array if no IPs were found or any of them were invalid
80:     def valid_ips
81:       WebhookEndpointValidator.ips_for_uri(url)
82:     end
83: 
84:     # Computes the HMAC signature for the given payload and secret.
85:     # https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks/using-webhooks/validating-webhook-deliveries
86:     def compute_signature
87:       'sha256=' + OpenSSL::HMAC.hexdigest(OpenSSL::Digest.new('sha256'), secret, payload)
88:     end
89:   end
90: 
91:   belongs_to :user
92:   has_and_belongs_to_many :projects # rubocop:disable Rails/HasAndBelongsToMany
93: 
94:   validates :url, presence: true, webhook_endpoint: true, length: { maximum: 2000 }
95:   validates :secret, length: { maximum: 255 }, allow_blank: true
96:   validate :check_events_array
97: 
98:   serialize :events, coder: YAML, type: Array
99: 
100:   scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }
101: 
102:   before_validation ->(hook){ hook.projects = hook.projects.to_a & hook.setable_projects }
103: 
104:   def self.enabled?
105:     Setting.webhooks_enabled?
106:   end
107: 
108:   # Triggers the given event for the given object, scheduling qualifying hooks
109:   # to be called.
110:   def self.trigger(event, object)
111:     return unless enabled?
112: 
113:     hooks_for(event, object).each do |hook|
114:       payload = hook.payload(event, object)
115:       WebhookJob.perform_later(hook.id, payload.to_json)
116:     end
117:   end
118: 
119:   # Finds hooks for the given event and object.
120:   # Returns an array of hooks that are active, have the given event in their list
121:   # of events, and whose user can see the object.
122:   #
123:   # Object must have a project_id and respond to visible?(user)
124:   def self.hooks_for(event, object)
125:     Webhook.active
126:       .joins("INNER JOIN projects_webhooks on projects_webhooks.webhook_id = webhooks.id")
127:       .eager_load(:user)
128:       .where(users: { status: User::STATUS_ACTIVE }, projects_webhooks: { project_id: object.project_id })
129:       .to_a.select do |hook|
130:       hook.events.include?(event) && object.visible?(hook.user) && hook.user.allowed_to?(:use_webhooks, object.project)
131:     end
132:   end
133: 
134:   def setable_projects
135:     user = self.user || User.current
136:     Project.visible(user).to_a.select{|p| user.allowed_to?(:use_webhooks, p)}
137:   end
138: 
139:   def setable_events
140:     WebhookPayload.events
141:   end
142: 
143:   def setable_event_names
144:     setable_events.map{|type, actions| actions.map{|action| "#{type}.#{action}"}}.flatten
145:   end
146: 
147:   # computes the payload. this happens when the hook is triggered, and the
148:   # payload is stored as part of the hook job definition.
149:   # event must be of the form 'type.action' (like 'issue.created')
150:   def payload(event, object)
151:     WebhookPayload.new(event, object, user).to_h
152:   end
153: 
154:   # POSTs the given payload to the hook URL, returns true if successful, false otherwise.
155:   #
156:   # logs any unsuccessful hook calls, but does not raise
157:   def call(payload_json)
158:     Executor.new(url, payload_json, secret).call
159:     true
160:   rescue => e
161:     Rails.logger.warn { "Webhook Error: #{e.message} (#{e.class})\n#{e.backtrace.join "\n"}" }
162:     false
163:   end
164: 
165:   private
166: 
167:   def check_events_array
168:     unless events.is_a?(Array)
169:       errors.add(:events, :invalid)
170:       return
171:     end
172: 
173:     events.reject!(&:blank?)
174:     if (events - setable_event_names).any?
175:       errors.add(:events, :invalid)
176:     end
177:   end
178: end

24:   def initialize(event, object, user)
25:     self.event = event
26:     self.object = object
27:     self.user = user
28:   end
29: 
30:   def self.register_model(model, model_events)
31:     raise ArgumentError, "model_events must be Array" unless model_events.is_a?(Array)
32: 
33:     @events ||= {}
34:     @events[model.model_name.singular.to_sym] = model_events
35:   end
36: 
37:   def self.events
38:     @events ||= {}
39:   end
40: 
41:   def to_h
42:     type, action = event.split('.')
43:     if self.class.events[type.to_sym]&.include?(action)
44:       object.webhook_payload(user, action)
45:     else
46:       raise ArgumentError, "invalid event: #{event}"
47:     end
48:   end

59:       self.path = path
60:       self.user = user
61:       self.params = params || {}
62:     end

65:       req = DummyRequest.new(params)
66:       api = Redmine::Views::Builders::Json.new(req, nil)
67:       ivars.each { |k, v| instance_variable_set :"@#{k}", v }
68:       original_user = User.current
69:       begin
70:         User.current = self.user
71:         instance_eval(File.read(Rails.root.join(path)), path, 1)
72:       ensure
73:         User.current = original_user
74:       end
75:     end
76:   end
77: end

36:     retrieve_query
37:     @query.group_by = nil
38:     @gantt.query = @query if @query.valid?
39: 
40:     basename = (@project ? "#{@project.identifier}-" : '') + 'gantt'
41:

43:       format.html {render :action => "show", :layout => !request.xhr?}
44:       if @gantt.respond_to?(:to_image)
45:         format.png do
46:           send_data(@gantt.to_image,
47:                     :disposition => 'inline', :type => 'image/png',
48:                     :filename => "#{basename}.png")
49:         end
50:       end
51:       format.pdf do

53:                   :type => 'application/pdf',

55:       end
